controls.title: Classification rules for high-risk AI systems
controls.description: Rules for determining which AI systems are classified as high-risk.

controls.title: Risk management system
controls.description: Providers of high-risk AI must implement a risk management system covering all reasonably foreseeable risks.

controls.title: Data and data governance
controls.description: Training, validation and testing data sets shall meet quality criteria including relevance, representativeness, and freedom from errors.

controls.title: Technical documentation
controls.description: Technical documentation shall be drawn up before placing on the market and kept up-to-date.

controls.title: Record-keeping
controls.description: High-risk AI systems shall allow automatic recording of events (logs).

controls.title: Transparency and provision of information to deployers
controls.description: High-risk AI systems shall be designed to ensure sufficient transparency for deployers.

controls.title: Human oversight
controls.description: High-risk AI systems shall be designed to allow effective oversight by natural persons.

controls.title: Accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity
controls.description: High-risk AI systems shall achieve appropriate levels of accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity.

controls.title: Obligations of providers of high-risk AI systems
controls.description: Providers shall ensure compliance with requirements, establish quality management, and maintain documentation.

controls.title: Quality management system
controls.description: Providers shall put a quality management system in place ensuring compliance with the regulation.

controls.title: Responsibilities along the AI value chain
controls.description: Distributors, importers, deployers, and other third parties have defined responsibilities.

controls.title: Obligations of deployers of high-risk AI systems
controls.description: Deployers shall use AI systems in accordance with instructions, ensure human oversight, and monitor operation.

controls.title: Fundamental rights impact assessments
controls.description: Deployers of high-risk AI shall carry out an assessment of impact on fundamental rights before use.

controls.title: Transparency obligations for certain AI systems
controls.description: AI systems that interact with people, generate deepfakes, or produce synthetic content must disclose this.

controls.title: Classification of GPAI models as systemic risk
controls.description: GPAI models with systemic risk are classified based on high-impact capabilities or cumulative compute thresholds.

controls.title: Obligations for providers of GPAI models
controls.description: All GPAI providers must prepare technical documentation, provide information to downstream providers, comply with copyright, and publish training data summaries.

controls.title: Authorised representatives of GPAI model providers
controls.description: GPAI providers outside the EU must appoint an authorised representative.

controls.title: Obligations of GPAI models with systemic risk
controls.description: Providers of GPAI with systemic risk must conduct model evaluations, assess and mitigate systemic risks, track incidents, and ensure adequate cybersecurity.

controls.title: Codes of practice
controls.description: The AI Office shall encourage development of codes of practice for GPAI obligations.

controls.title: AI regulatory sandboxes
controls.description: National authorities may establish regulatory sandboxes for development and testing of innovative AI.

controls.title: Reporting of serious incidents
controls.description: Providers and deployers shall report serious incidents to market surveillance authorities.

controls.title: Post-market monitoring by providers
controls.description: Providers shall establish a post-market monitoring system proportionate to the AI system.

controls.title: Reporting of serious incidents
controls.description: Detailed requirements for reporting serious incidents involving AI systems.

controls.title: High-risk AI systems
controls.description: List of areas where AI systems are classified as high-risk (biometrics, critical infrastructure, education, employment, essential services, law enforcement, migration, justice).

controls.title: Technical documentation
controls.description: Detailed requirements for technical documentation of high-risk AI systems.

changelog.change: Initial seed — 25 key articles and annexes from Regulation 2024/1689
changelog.author: emmanuelgjr
